Title: Capture a billable service charge from a completed encounter
Preconditions: Logged in as Billing Clerk; a completed clinical encounter exists for patient with at least one billable service order
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient billing dashboard | 2. Select the completed encounter | 3. Click Capture Charges | 4. Confirm the service line item appears | 5. Click Save
Test_Data: Patient=Jane Synthetic; Encounter=ENC-1001; Service=Office Visit Level 3
Expected_Result: The service is added as a billable line item with status Captured and is linked to the source encounter

Title: Reject charge capture when encounter is not finalized
Preconditions: Logged in as Billing Clerk; an encounter exists in Draft/In-Progress state
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient billing dashboard | 2. Select the in-progress encounter | 3. Click Capture Charges
Expected_Result: System blocks charge capture and displays a validation message that only finalized encounters can be charged; no line item is created

Title: Prevent duplicate charge capture for the same encounter service
Preconditions: Logged in as Billing Clerk; charges already captured for encounter ENC-1001
Test_Steps: 1. Open encounter ENC-1001 | 2. Click Capture Charges again | 3. Attempt to re-save the same service line
Test_Data: Patient=Jane Synthetic; Encounter=ENC-1001; Service=Office Visit Level 3
Expected_Result: System detects duplicate and prevents a second identical billable line, displaying a duplicate-charge warning

Title: Capture multiple distinct services on one encounter
Preconditions: Logged in as Billing Clerk; encounter has 3 distinct billable services
Test_Steps: 1. Open the encounter | 2. Click Capture Charges | 3. Verify all 3 services listed | 4. Save
Expected_Result: All three services are captured as separate line items each with its own price and code

Title: Charge capture writes an audit log entry with user and timestamp
Preconditions: DB access available; Billing Clerk session active
Test_Steps: 1. Capture a charge for encounter ENC-1004 | 2. Query the billing audit table | 3. Inspect the latest row
Expected_Result: An audit record exists capturing user id, action=CHARGE_CAPTURE, entity id, and UTC timestamp

Title: Apply current active price from the chargemaster to a captured service
Preconditions: Chargemaster has an active price for service code 99213; Billing Clerk logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Capture service 99213 | 2. Observe the unit price | 3. Compare to chargemaster active price
Expected_Result: The line item unit price equals the active chargemaster price of 120.00 and currency USD

Title: Use effective-dated price matching the date of service
Preconditions: Chargemaster has two price rows for 99213: effective until 31-Dec-2025=100.00 and from 01-Jan-2026=120.00
Test_Steps: 1. Capture 99213 on a service date of 15-Dec-2025 | 2. Capture 99213 on a service date of 15-Jan-2026 | 3. Compare applied prices
Expected_Result: First charge uses 100.00 and second uses 120.00 based on the effective-dated price selection

Title: Reject charge when no active price exists for the service code
Preconditions: Service code 88888 has no chargemaster entry; Billing Clerk logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to capture service 88888 | 2. Save
Expected_Result: System blocks save and reports that no active price is configured for the service code

Title: Boundary: price of exactly 0.00 for a no-charge service
Preconditions: Service code Z0000 configured with price 0.00 (no-charge); Billing Clerk logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Capture service Z0000 | 2. Verify line total
Expected_Result: Service is captured with a 0.00 line total and does not error; line is flagged as no-charge

Title: Boundary: maximum allowed price value precision and rounding
Preconditions: Chargemaster supports 2-decimal currency; Billing Clerk logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Configure price 99999999.999 | 2. Save chargemaster entry | 3. Capture and view line total
Expected_Result: Price is rounded/validated to 2 decimals (99999999.99 or rejection per config) with no silent truncation error

Title: Generate an invoice from captured charges
Preconditions: Patient has 3 captured charges not yet invoiced; Billing Clerk logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient billing dashboard | 2. Select the 3 captured charges | 3. Click Generate Invoice | 4. Confirm
Expected_Result: An invoice is created with a unique invoice number, all 3 line items, computed subtotal, and status Open

Title: Invoice subtotal equals sum of line items
Preconditions: Invoice INV-2001 generated with lines 120.00, 45.00, 30.00
Test_Steps: 1. Open invoice INV-2001 | 2. Read the subtotal field | 3. Sum line items manually
Expected_Result: Subtotal shown is exactly 195.00 matching the arithmetic sum of line items

Title: Prevent generating an invoice with zero line items
Preconditions: No charges selected; Billing Clerk logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open billing dashboard | 2. Click Generate Invoice without selecting charges
Expected_Result: System blocks invoice creation and prompts to select at least one charge

Title: Void an open invoice and release its charges
Preconditions: Invoice INV-2002 in Open status; Billing Supervisor logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open INV-2002 | 2. Click Void | 3. Enter void reason | 4. Confirm | 5. Verify charge status
Expected_Result: Invoice transitions Open to Voided, charges return to Captured/uninvoiced, and a void reason is recorded

Title: Block voiding an invoice that already has posted payments
Preconditions: Invoice INV-2003 has a posted payment of 50.00; Billing Supervisor logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open INV-2003 | 2. Click Void | 3. Confirm
Expected_Result: System prevents void and instructs to reverse/refund the payment first; invoice remains Open

Title: Invoice lifecycle Open to PartiallyPaid to Paid
Preconditions: Invoice INV-2004 subtotal 100.00 in Open status
Test_Steps: 1. Post payment 40.00 | 2. Verify status | 3. Post payment 60.00 | 4. Verify status
Expected_Result: After first payment status is PartiallyPaid with balance 60.00; after second status is Paid with balance 0.00

Title: Calculate patient co-pay for an in-network visit
Preconditions: Patient has active plan with 25.00 office-visit co-pay; in-network provider; Billing Clerk logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open the visit invoice | 2. Run co-pay calculation | 3. Read patient responsibility
Expected_Result: Patient responsibility co-pay is computed as 25.00 and shown as patient-due at point of service

Title: Apply higher out-of-network co-pay tier
Preconditions: Plan defines In-network copay 25.00 and Out-of-network copay 50.00
Test_Steps: 1. Set provider network=Out | 2. Run co-pay calculation | 3. Read co-pay
Expected_Result: Co-pay applied is 50.00 reflecting the out-of-network tier from the decision table

Title: Boundary: co-pay cannot exceed the visit charge
Preconditions: Plan copay 50.00; visit charge 30.00
Test_Steps: 1. Capture a 30.00 visit | 2. Apply 50.00 co-pay | 3. Verify patient-due
Expected_Result: Patient co-pay is capped at the visit charge (30.00) and never exceeds total charge

Title: Waive co-pay for preventive service per plan rule
Preconditions: Plan waives co-pay for preventive/wellness visits
Test_Steps: 1. Capture preventive visit code | 2. Run co-pay calculation
Expected_Result: Co-pay is 0.00 for the preventive service and a waiver reason is recorded

Title: Verify active insurance eligibility returns coverage active
Preconditions: Patient has a payer policy on file; eligibility service (270/271) reachable; Billing Clerk logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient coverage tab | 2. Click Verify Eligibility | 3. Wait for response | 4. Read coverage status
Expected_Result: Eligibility response returns Active coverage with plan name, effective dates, and copay/deductible details

Title: Handle inactive/terminated coverage response
Preconditions: Patient policy is terminated at payer; eligibility service reachable
Test_Steps: 1. Click Verify Eligibility | 2. Read response
Expected_Result: System shows coverage Inactive/Terminated and flags the patient as self-pay until coverage updated

Title: Gracefully handle eligibility service timeout
Preconditions: Eligibility endpoint configured but unreachable/slow; Billing Clerk logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Click Verify Eligibility | 2. Wait beyond configured timeout
Expected_Result: System surfaces a clear timeout error, does not crash, and allows manual retry without losing entry

Title: FHIR CoverageEligibilityRequest produces valid CoverageEligibilityResponse
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint exposes $submit for eligibility; API client authorized
Test_Steps: 1. POST a CoverageEligibilityRequest | 2. Capture the CoverageEligibilityResponse | 3. Validate against R4 profile
Test_Data: FHIR R4; Patient ref; Coverage ref
Expected_Result: Response is a valid R4 CoverageEligibilityResponse with outcome=complete and benefit balances populated

Title: HL7 270 eligibility inquiry maps to 271 response fields
Preconditions: X12 270/271 gateway configured; test member loaded
Test_Steps: 1. Build a 270 inquiry | 2. Submit to clearinghouse | 3. Parse the 271 response
Expected_Result: 271 returns eligibility benefit (EB) segments correctly mapped to plan, copay, and deductible in the UI

Title: Create a professional claim from a finalized invoice
Preconditions: Invoice INV-2005 finalized with diagnosis and procedure codes; payer on file
Test_Steps: 1. Open INV-2005 | 2. Click Create Claim | 3. Verify CPT, ICD-10, NPI, payer | 4. Save claim
Expected_Result: A claim is created in Draft with correct CPT/ICD-10/NPI/payer and a unique claim id

Title: Block claim creation when diagnosis code is missing
Preconditions: Invoice has procedure but no diagnosis linked; Billing Clerk logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Create claim from the invoice | 2. Attempt to submit/save
Expected_Result: System blocks creation and reports a required diagnosis code is missing for the procedure

Title: Validate NPI check digit on the rendering provider
Preconditions: Provider record exists with an invalid NPI; Billing Clerk logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Create claim | 2. Set rendering NPI to an invalid value | 3. Validate
Expected_Result: System rejects the invalid NPI via Luhn check-digit validation and prompts correction

Title: Submit a clean claim to the payer/clearinghouse
Preconditions: Claim CLM-3001 in Draft and validated; clearinghouse connection active
Test_Steps: 1. Open CLM-3001 | 2. Click Submit | 3. Confirm | 4. Read submission acknowledgment
Expected_Result: Claim transitions Draft to Submitted, a payer/clearinghouse acknowledgment (999/277CA) is recorded

Title: Generate X12 837P with required loops and segments
Preconditions: Claim validated; 837P generator configured
Test_Steps: 1. Generate 837P for CLM-3001 | 2. Inspect ISA/GS/ST envelope | 3. Verify 2000A/2010BA/2300 loops
Expected_Result: 837P contains valid envelope and required subscriber, patient, and claim loops passing SNIP level 2

Title: Reject submission of an already-submitted claim
Preconditions: Claim CLM-3001 already in Submitted status
Test_Steps: 1. Open CLM-3001 | 2. Click Submit again
Expected_Result: System prevents duplicate submission and shows the claim is already submitted

Title: Poll and display claim status from payer (277)
Preconditions: Claim CLM-3001 submitted; 276/277 status inquiry enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Open CLM-3001 | 2. Click Check Status | 3. Read returned status
Expected_Result: Claim status is updated from the 277 response (e.g., Accepted/In Process) with payer status code displayed

Title: Claim status transitions Submitted to Accepted to Paid
Preconditions: Claim CLM-3002 submitted; status updates available
Test_Steps: 1. Verify Submitted | 2. Apply Accepted update | 3. Apply Paid update | 4. Verify history
Expected_Result: Claim moves Submitted to Accepted to Paid and a full status-change history is retained

Title: Record a claim denial with CARC/RARC reason codes
Preconditions: Claim CLM-3003 returned denied on the 835; Billing Clerk logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open CLM-3003 | 2. Import/enter denial | 3. Verify CARC/RARC codes | 4. Read denial reason
Expected_Result: Claim status is Denied with CARC CO-16 and RARC N290 captured and human-readable reason shown

Title: Correct and resubmit a denied claim (rebill)
Preconditions: Claim CLM-3003 in Denied status with a correctable error
Test_Steps: 1. Open denied claim | 2. Correct the missing field | 3. Click Resubmit | 4. Verify new status
Expected_Result: Claim transitions Denied to Resubmitted with a corrected payload and a link to the original claim retained

Title: Distinguish a front-end rejection (277CA) from a denial
Preconditions: Claim CLM-3004 rejected at clearinghouse before adjudication
Test_Steps: 1. Open CLM-3004 | 2. View rejection detail | 3. Confirm classification
Expected_Result: System classifies the claim as Rejected (not Denied), shows the edit reason, and allows correction without appeal

Title: Post an insurance payment from an 835 ERA
Preconditions: 835 ERA file available for claim CLM-3002; Billing Clerk logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Import the 835 ERA | 2. Match to claim CLM-3002 | 3. Post payment | 4. Verify allowed/paid/adjustment
Expected_Result: Payment of 80.00 posts to the claim, contractual adjustment 20.00 recorded, and balance recalculated

Title: Post a patient cash payment against an invoice
Preconditions: Invoice INV-2006 Open with balance 75.00; Cashier logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open INV-2006 | 2. Click Post Payment | 3. Enter amount and method | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Payment posts, invoice balance becomes 0.00, status Paid, and a receipt number is generated

Title: Reject overpayment exceeding outstanding balance
Preconditions: Invoice INV-2007 balance 50.00; Cashier logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open INV-2007 | 2. Post payment 80.00 | 3. Save
Expected_Result: System blocks or routes the 30.00 overage to credit handling; never creates a negative due silently

Title: Boundary: post a payment of the exact remaining balance
Preconditions: Invoice INV-2008 balance 33.33; Cashier logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open INV-2008 | 2. Post payment 33.33 | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Invoice balance becomes exactly 0.00, status Paid, with no rounding residue

Title: Reject negative or zero payment amount
Preconditions: Invoice INV-2009 Open; Cashier logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open INV-2009 | 2. Enter payment amount -10.00 | 3. Save | 4. Retry with 0.00
Expected_Result: System rejects both negative and zero payment amounts with a validation error

Title: Auto-post ERA across multiple claims with partial matching
Preconditions: 835 ERA contains payments for matched, partially-matched, and unmatched claims
Test_Steps: 1. Import multi-claim 835 | 2. Run auto-post | 3. Review match results
Expected_Result: Matched claims auto-post; partial and unmatched route to a manual worklist; totals reconcile to 835 control sum

Title: Apply a contractual adjustment to a claim line
Preconditions: Claim line charged 100.00; allowed 70.00; Billing Clerk logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open the claim line | 2. Enter contractual adjustment | 3. Save | 4. Verify balance
Expected_Result: A 30.00 contractual adjustment is recorded, patient/payer balance updated to allowed amount

Title: Write-off small balance under a configured threshold
Preconditions: Write-off rule: balances under 5.00 auto-write-off; balance 2.50 exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open invoice with 2.50 balance | 2. Run small-balance write-off
Expected_Result: Balance is written off to 0.00 with reason Small Balance Write-off and audit entry created

Title: Issue a refund for an overpaid invoice
Preconditions: Invoice INV-2010 overpaid by 25.00 (credit balance); Billing Supervisor logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open INV-2010 | 2. Click Issue Refund | 3. Enter 25.00 and method | 4. Approve | 5. Save
Expected_Result: A refund of 25.00 is recorded, credit balance cleared, and refund requires supervisor approval

Title: Reverse a posted payment and restore the invoice balance
Preconditions: Invoice INV-2012 Paid with a posted 60.00 payment; Billing Supervisor logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open INV-2012 | 2. Click Reverse Payment | 3. Enter reversal reason | 4. Approve | 5. Verify status and balance
Expected_Result: Payment is reversed, invoice returns from Paid to Open with balance 60.00, and a reversal audit entry is recorded

Title: Block refund exceeding available credit balance
Preconditions: Invoice INV-2011 credit balance 10.00; Billing Supervisor logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open INV-2011 | 2. Issue refund 40.00 | 3. Save
Expected_Result: System rejects the refund because it exceeds the 10.00 available credit balance

Title: Decision table: primary vs secondary payer coordination of benefits
Preconditions: Patient has primary and secondary coverage; claim total 200.00
Test_Steps: 1. Adjudicate primary | 2. Calculate remaining | 3. Submit to secondary | 4. Verify patient residual
Expected_Result: Primary pays 150.00, secondary 40.00, patient residual 10.00; COB order applied correctly

Title: Decision table: deductible not met routes balance to patient
Preconditions: Plan deductible 500.00 with 0.00 met; service allowed 120.00
Test_Steps: 1. Adjudicate the claim | 2. Apply deductible logic | 3. Read patient responsibility
Expected_Result: Full 120.00 is applied to deductible as patient responsibility; payer pays 0.00

Title: Decision table: coinsurance after deductible met
Preconditions: Deductible met; plan coinsurance 20%; allowed 200.00
Test_Steps: 1. Adjudicate claim | 2. Apply 80/20 coinsurance | 3. Read splits
Expected_Result: Payer pays 160.00 and patient owes 40.00 coinsurance after deductible is met

Title: Decision table: out-of-pocket maximum reached zeroes patient share
Preconditions: Patient OOP max 6000.00 already reached; new allowed 300.00
Test_Steps: 1. Adjudicate new claim | 2. Apply OOP-max rule | 3. Read patient share
Expected_Result: Patient owes 0.00 and payer covers full 300.00 because OOP max is reached

Title: Pairwise: network x service-type x auth-status combinations
Preconditions: Coverage engine supports network (In/Out), service type (Office/ER/Imaging), auth (Yes/No/NA)
Test_Steps: 1. Run engine across selected pairwise combinations | 2. Compare each to expected matrix
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination produces the expected coverage, copay, and auth-required outcome

Title: Apply sales/health tax to a taxable service line
Preconditions: Tax rule: taxable service code S100 at 8.5%; Billing Clerk logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Capture taxable service S100 at 100.00 | 2. Generate invoice | 3. Read tax line
Expected_Result: Invoice shows tax of 8.50 and total 108.50 computed on the taxable line only

Title: Exempt non-taxable clinical service from tax
Preconditions: Tax rule: clinical service C200 is tax-exempt
Test_Steps: 1. Capture exempt service C200 at 100.00 | 2. Generate invoice | 3. Read tax line
Expected_Result: No tax is applied to the exempt service; invoice total equals 100.00

Title: Boundary: tax rounding at half-cent threshold
Preconditions: Tax rate 8.5% on 99.99; rounding=half-up
Test_Steps: 1. Capture 99.99 taxable | 2. Compute tax
Expected_Result: Tax computes to 8.50 (8.49915 rounded half-up) with consistent currency rounding

Title: Generate a patient statement for outstanding balance
Preconditions: Patient has invoices with a 130.00 patient-responsibility balance; Billing Clerk logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient account | 2. Click Generate Statement | 3. Review line items and balance
Expected_Result: Statement is generated showing dated charges, payments, adjustments, and a 130.00 amount due

Title: Statement masks full member ID and shows only last 4
Preconditions: Patient statement contains insurance member identifier
Test_Steps: 1. Generate statement | 2. Inspect rendered member ID
Expected_Result: Statement shows only the last 4 of the member id (****6789); full id is masked for PHI safety

Title: Suppress statement when balance is zero or credit
Preconditions: Patient account balance is 0.00; statement run scheduled
Test_Steps: 1. Run statement batch | 2. Check whether a statement is produced for the zero-balance account
Expected_Result: No statement is generated for a zero or credit balance per dunning policy

Title: Display Explanation of Benefits parsed from an 835
Preconditions: 835 ERA posted for claim CLM-3002; Billing Clerk logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open claim CLM-3002 | 2. Click View EOB | 3. Review billed, allowed, paid, adjustment, patient resp
Expected_Result: EOB displays billed 100.00, allowed 70.00, paid 56.00, adjustment 30.00, and patient responsibility 14.00

Title: Reconcile EOB patient responsibility to patient statement
Preconditions: EOB patient responsibility 14.00 posted; patient statement generated
Test_Steps: 1. Read EOB patient responsibility | 2. Generate statement | 3. Compare amounts
Expected_Result: Patient statement amount due reflects the 14.00 EOB patient responsibility with no discrepancy

Title: Enforce role-based access to refund function
Preconditions: User with Billing Clerk role (no refund permission) logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open an invoice with credit | 2. Attempt to issue a refund
Expected_Result: Refund action is hidden/blocked for the clerk; only authorized supervisor roles can refund; attempt is audit-logged

Title: Prevent IDOR access to another patient's invoice
Preconditions: Logged in as Billing Clerk scoped to Location A; invoice belongs to Location B patient
Test_Steps: 1. Note own invoice URL/id | 2. Manipulate id to another patient invoice | 3. Submit request
Expected_Result: System denies access to the unauthorized invoice (403) and logs the attempt; no PHI is returned

Title: Reject SQL injection in invoice search field
Preconditions: Billing search screen available; Billing Clerk logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Enter an injection payload in invoice search | 2. Submit
Expected_Result: Input is safely parameterized; no data leak or error; search returns no matches and event is logged

Title: REST API creates a charge and returns 201 with resource id
Preconditions: API credentials with billing scope; service endpoint available
Test_Steps: 1. POST a charge payload to /billing/charges | 2. Read status and body
Expected_Result: API returns 201 Created with a charge id and echoes the persisted line item fields

Title: API rejects charge create without authentication token
Preconditions: API endpoint requires bearer token
Test_Steps: 1. POST to /billing/charges with no Authorization header
Expected_Result: API returns 401 Unauthorized and does not create any charge

Title: API enforces idempotency key on payment posting
Preconditions: Payment API supports Idempotency-Key header
Test_Steps: 1. POST a payment with Idempotency-Key K1 | 2. Retry identical POST with K1
Expected_Result: Second identical request returns the original result without creating a duplicate payment

Title: Generate a FHIR Claim resource that validates against R4
Preconditions: FHIR R4 server available; claim data complete
Test_Steps: 1. Build a FHIR Claim resource | 2. POST to the FHIR server | 3. Validate against R4 Claim profile
Expected_Result: Server accepts the resource (201) and it passes R4 Claim profile validation with required elements present

Title: FHIR ClaimResponse adjudication amounts map to local EOB
Preconditions: FHIR ClaimResponse returned for a submitted Claim
Test_Steps: 1. Retrieve the ClaimResponse | 2. Read adjudication slices | 3. Map to local EOB fields
Expected_Result: Submitted, eligible, benefit, and patient adjudication amounts map correctly to the local EOB display

Title: Referential integrity between payment and invoice on delete
Preconditions: DB access available; payment row references invoice INV-2006
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to hard-delete invoice INV-2006 with a posted payment | 2. Observe DB constraint
Expected_Result: Database/foreign-key constraint prevents orphaning the payment; delete is blocked or cascades per policy

Title: Monetary fields stored as exact decimal not float
Preconditions: DB access available; sample invoice with 0.10+0.20 lines
Test_Steps: 1. Insert line items 0.10 and 0.20 | 2. Query stored subtotal | 3. Inspect data type
Expected_Result: Subtotal stored is exactly 0.30 using decimal/numeric type with no floating-point drift

Title: Invoice and payment screens meet WCAG 2.1 AA keyboard and label rules
Preconditions: Billing UI loaded; screen reader and keyboard available
Test_Steps: 1. Tab through invoice form | 2. Verify focus order and labels | 3. Check color contrast | 4. Run axe scan
Expected_Result: All controls are keyboard reachable with visible focus, labeled for screen readers, and meet 4.5:1 contrast

Title: Statement PDF is tagged and screen-reader navigable
Preconditions: Generated statement PDF available; screen reader available
Test_Steps: 1. Open statement PDF | 2. Verify tag structure and reading order | 3. Read amounts via screen reader
Expected_Result: PDF is tagged with logical reading order and the amount due is announced correctly by the screen reader

Title: Exploratory session on partial payments, voids, and refunds interplay
Preconditions: Test account with several invoices in mixed states; Billing Supervisor logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Freely combine partial payment, void, adjustment, and refund actions | 2. Observe balances and states | 3. Note anomalies
Expected_Result: No inconsistent balances, negative dues, or orphaned charges arise; all transitions remain auditable

Title: Exploratory time-zone and date-of-service boundary on pricing
Preconditions: Pricing effective-dated near midnight UTC boundary; tester across time zones
Test_Steps: 1. Capture charges around the effective-date boundary in different time zones | 2. Compare applied prices
Expected_Result: Effective-dated pricing resolves consistently to date of service regardless of client time zone

Title: Boundary: claim with maximum allowed line items submits successfully
Preconditions: Payer/format allows up to 50 service lines per claim
Test_Steps: 1. Build a claim with exactly 50 lines | 2. Validate | 3. Submit | 4. Attempt 51 lines
Expected_Result: 50-line claim submits successfully; the 51st line triggers split-claim logic or a clear limit message

Title: Pairwise: payer x plan-type x relationship eligibility combinations
Preconditions: Eligibility engine supports multiple payers, plan types (HMO/PPO/HDHP), and relationships (Self/Spouse/Child)
Test_Steps: 1. Run pairwise combinations through eligibility | 2. Compare each to expected benefits
Expected_Result: Each combination returns the correct active/inactive status and benefit set per the pairwise matrix
